title: How to convert a score to a letter grade
task: Map numeric scores to A/B/C/D/F (or any tiered label) automatically.

excel.note: Excel 2019+. Older Excel: nested IFs, or the VLOOKUP approach in the explanation.

explanation: IFS checks the conditions in order and returns the first hit: 84 fails the ≥90 test but passes ≥80, so it's a B. The TRUE,"F" pair at the end is the catch-all — without it, scores below 60 return #N/A. For long or changing scales, a lookup table beats a formula: thresholds 0/60/70/80/90 in one column, letters in the next, then the closest-match lookup pattern (=VLOOKUP(A2,table,2,TRUE)) — edit the table, not the formula.
